query: European Commission DMA non-compliance decision against Google Search Article 6(5) self-preferencing July 2026 - has a formal decision been adopted or announced? Latest status as of mid-July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Bottom line:** As of mid-July 2026, **no formal DMA non-compliance decision against Google Search for Article 6(5) self-preferencing has been publicly confirmed as adopted or announced.** All available reporting indicates the decision remains **pending/imminent** — described as "nearing completion" and "expected before the EU's summer parliamentary recess" (late July/early August).

**Key findings:**
- The expected fine is in the **"high triple-digit million euro" range** (>€500M, <€1B) — would be the largest DMA penalty to date.
- Non-compliance proceedings opened 25 March 2024; Article 6(5) self-preferencing charges issued March 2025 (Google favoring Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Maps over rivals).
- Latest concrete reporting is from **late May 2026** (Handelsblatt/Reuters/CNBC), with final approval reportedly resting with President von der Leyen. The announcement was repeatedly delayed amid US–EU trade tensions.
- A **distinct** case — the older €4.1B Android antitrust fine — was upheld by the EU's top court on 2 July 2026, which should not be confused with the DMA self-preferencing decision.

**Caveat:** My search coverage was strong through early July 2026 but I could not definitively rule out a decision adopted in the narrow July 7–14 window; no accessed source confirmed one, and no EC press-corner release announcing it was found.

key_facts: As of mid-July 2026, no formal EU DMA non-compliance decision against Google Search for self-preferencing (Article 6(5)) has been publicly confirmed as adopted; it remains pending/imminent.

Latest substantive reporting (late May 2026, via Handelsblatt/Reuters/CNBC) described the decision as 'nearing completion' and expected 'before the summer parliamentary recess' (late July/early August 2026).
The expected fine is in the 'high triple-digit million euro' range (>€500M, <€1B), which would be the largest DMA penalty to date.
Non-compliance proceedings were opened 25 March 2024 (IP/24/1689); Article 6(5) self-preferencing charges/preliminary findings were issued in March 2025.
The case concerns Google allegedly ranking its own vertical services (Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Maps) above rival comparison/vertical-search platforms.
Final approval reportedly rested with Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, who was still deliberating as of late May 2026.
The announcement was reportedly delayed for months amid geopolitical/US trade tensions over EU Big Tech fines.
On 15 March 2026, 18 European industry/consumer groups sent an open letter urging a formal non-compliance decision before the 25 March 2026 two-year mark.

Separate/distinct matters: DMA.100209 search data-sharing preliminary measures (adopted 16 April 2026); specification proceedings opened 27 Jan 2026 (IP/26/202); Nov 2025 publisher-demotion probe (IP/25/2675).
On 2–3 July 2026 the EU's top court upheld the older €4.1B ($4.67B) Android antitrust fine — a distinct, non-DMA case not to be confused with the self-preferencing DMA decision.
